economic_notes: First Nation has investments in Exchange Income Corporation ($1,000,000), Wasaya Airways Limited Partnership, Wasaya Group Inc., Ontario First Nation Limited Partnership, and a deposit toward purchase of L.K.G.H. Group of Companies sawmill assets. Eshkotay Power Generation wound up in 2021. Whitefeather Forest Management Corporation is a consolidated entity.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to improper deferral of revenue - auditors note that had revenue not been deferred, total revenue would increase by $22,264,741, deferred revenue would decrease by $41,073,594, and accumulated surplus would increase by $41,073,594. The deferred revenue balance of $47,598,591 is very large relative to total revenues. Four entities are fully consolidated: Eshkotay Wayab Corporation, Pikangikum Health Authority, Pikangikum CMHC Housing Authority, and Whitefeather Forest Management Corporation. CMHC mortgages cover 36 housing units (7+10+9+10). Audit report dated October 18, 2023 for March 31, 2022 year-end - significant delay. First Nation is not in compliance with First Nations Financial Transparency Act filing deadline. OSR calculation is negative because deferred revenue adjustments make government transfers appear to exceed total reported revenue; actual own-source revenues include fuel sales, hotel, lumber, rental, user fees etc. totalling approximately $7.1M. Total government transfers calculated as sum of all federal and provincial government transfer lines before deferred revenue adjustments.
